questions: The brief says to prefetch 'tool settings / the timelines list' when navigating — I'm planning to warm the settings cache via useToolSettings inside the always-mounted ToolsPane and append ?timeline=<id> at click time. Is that acceptable, or do you also want an explicit timelines-list prefetch (e.g., queryClient.prefetchQuery on hover)?

If the cached lastTimelineId points at a deleted/invalid timeline, VideoEditorProvider will fail to load it and currently there is no in-editor error fallback separate from the page-level loading card. Is the pre-existing provider-level failure path acceptable, or do you want an additional guard (e.g., don't seed from settings until timelines.data has confirmed the id exists)?

For the Opt+Shift+W global shortcut, should we accept that when the React Query cache is cold we simply navigate without the param (relying on Step 1 to seed from settings on the page), or do you want the shortcut to perform an async prefetch before navigating?

success_criteria.criterion: VideoEditorPage no longer renders the 'Opening video editor / Loading your latest timeline…' Card; that branch returns null instead.
The auto-select effect in VideoEditorPage seeds ?timeline=<id> from settings.lastTimelineId before consulting timelines.data, and no longer waits on timelines.isLoading to do so.
The timelines.error branch in VideoEditorPage still renders its 'Unable to open video editor' Card unchanged.
Navigation from the ToolsPane tool card to /tools/video-editor appends ?timeline=<lastTimelineId> when a cached value is available.
Opt+Shift+W global shortcut appends ?timeline=<lastTimelineId> when the value is in the React Query cache.
VideoEditorPage still auto-creates a default timeline on first project visit (no regression to the auto-create flow).
Lastly persisted timeline id continues to be written back to settings when the user opens a timeline (existing effect at 202–208 still fires).
pnpm tsc --noEmit passes with no new type errors.
No new imports of loading/Skeleton components left dangling in VideoEditorPage after removing the loading card.
Manual smoke: opening the Video Editor from the ToolsPane on a returning project shows the editor shell directly with no placeholder flash.
The refactor to hoist editor chrome out of VideoEditorProvider is NOT performed.

assumptions: The React Query cache key used by useToolSettings is stable and can be peeked from useGlobalPaneShortcuts via queryClient.getQueryData without triggering a fetch.
ToolsPane is always mounted once the user is authenticated into a project, so subscribing to useToolSettings there is effectively a session-long prefetch with negligible cost.

If settings.lastTimelineId points at a deleted timeline, the existing VideoEditorProvider/useTimelinesList flow will surface that via its own error handling — we do not need to add a new guard here (pre-existing behavior, out of scope).

The loading card replacement to `return null` is acceptable even in the rare case where selectedProjectId + userId are present but settings and timelines are both still loading from a cold cache — the blank frame will be brief and matches the brief's explicit instruction.
`videoEditorSettings.id` is importable in ToolsPane without creating a circular dependency (tools → shared is already used elsewhere e.g. toolManifest imports videoEditorSettings).
No existing unit test asserts the presence of the 'Opening video editor' Card; if one exists, it will be updated to assert the null/blank state instead.
